stepAnswer: Parameter: The proportion of all UC Berkeley students drinking < 3L of water per day; Statistic: proportion of the $$150$$ sampled students drinking < 3L of water per day
stepTitle: B) What is the parameter and what is the statistic in this scenario?
stepBody: You are interested in investigating the liters of water consumed every day by UC Berkeley students. In particular, you want to study the proportion of students drinking less than $$3$$ liters of water per day. You contact $$150$$ random students from the directory and obtain the amounts of water each one of them drinks, storing them in the table water. The table has $$1$$ column, amount, which stores the number of liters of water drunk by each student.
answerLatex: Parameter: The proportion of all UC Berkeley students drinking < 3L of water per day; Statistic: proportion of the $$150$$ sampled students drinking < 3L of water per day
choices: Parameter: The proportion of the $$150$$ sampled students drinking < 3L of water per day; Statistic: The proportion of all UC Berkeley students drinking < 3L of water per day
Parameter: The proportion of all UC Berkeley students drinking < 3L of water per day; Statistic: proportion of the $$150$$ sampled students drinking < 3L of water per day
